pattern: Changing one element only shifts which pivots balance, so tracking how a pivot's balance value would shift under that change (split into pivots before vs. after the changed index) turns an O(n^2) search into a single linear sweep.
hints: First count how many pivots already balance the array with no changes at all — that is a baseline you can only improve on.
For a pivot p, define its imbalance as twice the left-side sum minus the total; changing one element only shifts that imbalance for pivots strictly on one side of the changed index.

Precompute imbalance values for every pivot into a multiset. Sweep the changed index left to right, keeping a 'pivots before me' multiset and a 'pivots after me' multiset; for the change at i (delta = k - nums[i]), the answer candidate is count(delta) in the before-set plus count(-delta) in the after-set, then move pivot i+1 from after to before.

edge_inputs.description: changing to k has no effect since it matches the existing value
extreme constraint values for nums and k
only the last element change creates a balance point

stress.description: random array and k within the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    nums = [rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(-100000, 100000)
    return [nums, k]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def waysToPartition(self, nums, k):
        from collections import defaultdict
        n = len(nums)
        total = sum(nums)
        prefix = [0] * (n + 1)
        for idx in range(n):
            prefix[idx + 1] = prefix[idx] + nums[idx]
        right_count = defaultdict(int)
        for p in range(1, n):
            val = 2 * prefix[p] - total
            right_count[val] += 1
        base = right_count.get(0, 0)
        left_count = defaultdict(int)
        ans = base
        for i in range(n):
            delta = k - nums[i]
            cur = left_count.get(delta, 0) + right_count.get(-delta, 0)
            if cur > ans:
                ans = cur
            p = i + 1
            if p <= n - 1:
                val = 2 * prefix[p] - total
                right_count[val] -= 1
                left_count[val] += 1
        return ans
